text: The process of heating ore below its melting point in the presence of air to remove volatile impurities is called:
explanation: Roasting: heating sulfide/other ores in excess air below melting point. Converts sulfides to oxides: \( 2\text{ZnS} + 3\text{O}_2 \to 2\text{ZnO} + 2\text{SO}_2 \).
name: Roasting

text: The process of heating carbonate or hydrated oxide ores in absence of air to remove CO₂ or water is:
name: Calcination

text: The Ellingham diagram is used to:
options: Predict feasibility of reduction of metal oxides by carbon or CO
explanation: Ellingham diagram: plots \(\Delta G^\circ\) vs T for metal oxide formation. Used to find temperature at which carbon/CO can reduce a given oxide.
name: Ellingham Diagram

text: The Hall-Heroult process is used for extraction of:
explanation: Hall-Heroult process: electrolytic reduction of Al₂O₃ (dissolved in molten cryolite Na₃AlF₆) to obtain Al. 2Al₂O₃ → 4Al + 3O₂.
name: Hall-Heroult Process

text: Van Arkel method is used for refining:
explanation: Van Arkel method: Ti or Zr converted to volatile iodide (TiI₄), then thermally decomposed on tungsten filament to give pure metal.
name: Van Arkel Method

text: The process of obtaining the metal from concentrated ore by heating with reducing agent is:
explanation: Smelting: reduction of metal oxide with coke/CO at high temperature. E.g., \(\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3 + 3\text{CO} \to 2\text{Fe} + 3\text{CO}_2\).
name: Smelting

text: Froth flotation method separates ore from gangue based on:
explanation: Froth flotation: sulfide ores preferentially wetted by oil (not water) and float as froth. Gangue (silicates) wetted by water and settle.
name: Froth Flotation

text: Mond's process is used for refining of:
explanation: Mond's process for Ni: Ni + 4CO → Ni(CO)₄ (volatile, at 50-60°C), then Ni(CO)₄ → Ni + 4CO (at 230°C). Very pure Ni obtained.
name: Mond's Process

text: Cupellation is a refining process used for:
explanation: Cupellation: Ag/Au-Pb alloy is heated in a cupel (porous vessel). Pb oxidises to PbO (absorbed), leaving pure Ag or Au.
name: Cupellation

text: The liquation method is suitable for refining metals with:
options: Low melting points compared to impurities
explanation: Liquation: metal (e.g., Sn, Bi, Pb) with low melting point melts and flows away from high-melting impurities on an inclined surface.
name: Liquation

text: In the extraction of iron in a blast furnace, limestone is added as:
options: Flux to remove SiO₂ as slag
explanation: Limestone (CaCO₃) → CaO + CO₂. CaO + SiO₂ → CaSiO₃ (slag). Flux removes acidic gangue (silica).
name: Role of Limestone in Blast Furnace

text: Zone refining is based on the principle that:
options: Impurities are more soluble in molten metal than in solid metal
explanation: Zone refining: impurities concentrate in molten zone (higher solubility in liquid than solid). Used for ultra-pure Si, Ge for semiconductors.
name: Zone Refining

explanation: Bauxite (Al₂O₃·2H₂O or AlOOH) is the main ore of aluminium. Purified by Baeyer's process to get pure Al₂O₃.
name: Ore of Aluminium

options: Impure metal as cathode, pure metal as anode
Impure metal as anode, pure metal as cathode
explanation: Electrolytic refining: impure metal = anode (dissolves), pure metal = cathode (deposits). Impurities remain as anode mud.
name: Electrolytic Refining

text: The thermite reaction used in welding is:
explanation: Thermite reaction: \( 2\text{Al} + \text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3 \to \text{Al}_2\text{O}_3 + 2\text{Fe} + \Delta \). Highly exothermic (~3000°C). Used to weld railway tracks.
name: Thermite Reaction
